new_bridge_path.summary: A second concrete path packages client +0x19A20 into a compact endpoint descriptor object and later formats it into a scheme-plus-host-plus-port string.

snapshot_builder.role: Compact endpoint-descriptor builder that stores a live pointer back to client +0x19A20 together with caller-supplied numeric metadata and one string field.

notable_negative_result.observation: This snapshot builder does not directly expose the embedded object field [client +0x19A20] +0x2C / raw client +0x19A4C. The unresolved scalar remains separate from this descriptor's own mode dword at snapshot +0xC.

builder_callsite.observation: A higher-level caller allocates a wrapper object, invokes 0x14015D2B0 with rdx = client +0x19A20, stores the resulting endpoint descriptor at wrapper +0x18, and then installs that wrapper through 0x1400A8090.

formatter.role: Formats the endpoint descriptor into a textual scheme-plus-host-plus-port string.

decision_logic.observation: The formatter reads a byte flag from [root +0x468], where root is the stored client +0x19A20 pointer.
When that flag is nonzero, the formatter uses snapshot word +0x2A as the numeric port component and chooses the https:// literal.
When the flag is zero and root +0x10 equals the selector-table base 0x140C415A0, the formatter forces numeric value 0x50.
Otherwise the formatter derives the numeric component from snapshot dword +0x8, choosing between 0x1B58 + value and 0x2EE0 + value depending on whether snapshot dword +0xC equals 1.
The final string is formatted with either http:// or https://, the copied host string at snapshot +0x10, and the numeric component described above.

formatter.bounded_inference: This helper formats an endpoint or service URL stem rather than a full launcher URL, because the output is explicitly %s%s:%u with scheme, host, and numeric port only.

downstream_consumers.observation: A consumer reads client +0x19970, follows slot +0x20, formats that descriptor through 0x14015D330, and copies the resulting string into its output buffer through 0x140032150.

A second consumer reads client +0x19970, follows slot +0x68, formats that descriptor through 0x14015D330, and mirrors the resulting string into object field +0x728. If the slot is absent, it falls back to the shared empty-string literal at 0x140B5DCB8.

A larger composite builder also consumes 0x14015D330 output, then immediately formats another %s:%u string using 0x140B69720. This confirms the endpoint string participates in a broader bootstrap or launcher assembly path, though the full surrounding object is not yet named.

preserved_points: Client +0x19A20 remains the embedded parsed config object updated by 0x140033F90.
Client +0x19970 remains a separate subsystem with multiple stored descriptors or slots.
new_points: Client +0x19A20 is now proven to feed a second concrete transport path beyond the earlier record snapshots: an endpoint descriptor family that stores a live root pointer back to the embedded object.
Two client +0x19970 slots, at least +0x20 and +0x68, now have concrete downstream meaning as endpoint-descriptor sources for formatted strings.

updated_conclusion: A second concrete bridge from client +0x19A20 is now mapped. Helper 0x14015D2B0 builds a compact descriptor that stores a root pointer back to the embedded config object together with one host string and several numeric fields, and formatter 0x14015D330 turns that descriptor into a textual endpoint using either http:// or https:// plus a host and computed port. The formatter consults both the root object's selector field at +0x10 and a later flag at root +0x468, then surfaces the result through at least two client +0x19970 slots that are copied into downstream strings. This materially strengthens the bootstrap model because client +0x19A20 is no longer only a parsed config bundle with script and launcher mirrors; it is also a live input to endpoint formatting and transport setup.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: The client-19970-pair-subsystem-separation artifact is strengthened with concrete semantics for two stored slots that now format endpoint strings.
The client-19a20 derived-field and jav-config-refresh artifacts are strengthened because the same embedded object now has a proven second transport-facing consumer family.

The unresolved scalar at raw client +0x19A4C remains open; this pass narrows the gap by showing that the nearby descriptor-mode dword at snapshot +0xC is separate and should not be conflated with the embedded object's +0x2C field.
next_targets: Find the first direct consumer of the embedded object field [client +0x19A20] +0x2C / raw client +0x19A4C.
Name the root flag at [client +0x19A20] +0x468 and determine why it forces https:// plus snapshot word +0x2A in the endpoint formatter.

Classify the two client +0x19970 endpoint slots more precisely by tracing the callsites that populate +0x20 versus +0x68 and determining whether they represent different services, world endpoints, or launcher channels.
